title: Guardian
description: A guardian appointment for a minor child. Supports testamentary, court-appointed, shariah court, community-appointed, and religious court appointments. Guardianship structures include individual, collective, rotating, and family council determined.

id.description: Unique identifier for this guardian appointment within the INHERIT document.

personId.description: Reference to the Person.id of the guardian being appointed.

childPersonId.description: Reference to the Person.id of the child this guardian is appointed for.

role.$comment: primary: the first-choice guardian. secondary: acts alongside the primary or takes over if primary cannot act. substitute: named replacement if primary and secondary cannot act.
role.description: The guardian's priority in the appointment.

appointmentType.$comment: testamentary: appointed by the parent in their will. parental_responsibility: appointed by a parent with parental responsibility under statute (e.g. Children Act 1989 s.5). court_appointed: appointed by a secular court. shariah_court_appointed: appointed by a Shariah court — Islamic guardianship (hadanah/wilayah). community_appointed: appointed by a community body (e.g. family council in West Africa). religious_court_appointed: appointed by a religious court other than Shariah (e.g. beth din).

guardianshipStructure.$comment: individual: one named guardian. collective: multiple guardians acting jointly. rotating: guardianship rotates among family members (common in some African customary systems). family_council_determined: a family council determines the guardian — the will names the mechanism rather than the person.
guardianshipStructure.description: The structure of the guardianship arrangement.

familyCouncilDecisionRef.$comment: Reference to a family council decision document or record.
familyCouncilDecisionRef.description: Reference to the family council decision that appointed this guardian, if the appointment was council-determined.
examples: FC/2025/001 — Ogbuagu family council decision of 15 March 2025

conditions.description: Conditions on the guardianship — when it takes effect, any restrictions, or circumstances under which it lapses.
examples: Takes effect only if both parents die before the child reaches 18
Guardian must maintain the child's existing school placement

notes.description: Free-text notes about this guardianship appointment.
examples: Guardian lives in Canada — may need to apply for child's visa
Both parents wish the child to be raised in the Islamic faith
Guardian Auntie Lakshmi confirmed — child to continue at current school in Mumbai
Kwame Mensah agreed to act as guardian — family council approved
